meta.title: In-Section Activity Content Specification
meta.description: One interactive activity per content section for all nine curriculum modules. Each activity inserts immediately after the section body and before the Next button. Completable in under 60 seconds.

design_constraints.tests: Core concept of the section only — not peripheral details
design_constraints.answer_derivability: Correct answer unambiguously derivable from the section text just read
design_constraints.state_management: React state only — no API calls

implementation_note: Each activity renders as a card below the section body and above the Next button. A Skip ghost button allows bypass. Correct answers are revealed on submission and logged to the engagement tracker (key pattern: engagement:module[N]:activity:[section-id]).

modules.title: Why Skills Matter: The Problem With Prompts

activities.instruction: Classify each scenario as a cause of prompt brittleness or NOT a cause, before continuing.

items.text: A colleague edits a shared prompt to fix one use case.
A model vendor releases an update that changes output format subtly.
A new team member asks the same question using different phrasing.
The AI system is deployed in a new country.
An autonomous agent calls the same prompt hundreds of times.

correct_answers.explanation: Geographic deployment is not cited as a brittleness cause in this section.

activities.section_title: The Skill Primitive: A Better Abstraction
activities.instruction: Match each concept to its correct analogy before continuing.

analogies.text: A complex behavior condensed into a single accessible unit
A conversation written for human judgment, not machine contracts
A software function with defined inputs, outputs, and edge cases

pairs.explanation: brittle prompt = human conversation, no contract

activities.instruction: Identify the specific flaw in this routing description before continuing.

items.text: description: "You are a helpful assistant. When asked about competitors, please provide a useful and thorough analysis covering things like their pricing, what their product does, and any weaknesses you know about. Try to be comprehensive and helpful."

flaws: No concrete trigger phrases — 'When asked about competitors' is too vague for reliable routing.
No output shape specified — no schema, column count, or structure for the output.
No deterministic contract — 'be comprehensive and helpful' varies every run.

activities.instruction: Cover the section and reconstruct the correct directory structure for a skills repository before continuing.
prompts: What are the required elements of a skill directory?
Where are skills stored, and why does that location matter?

correct_answers.required_elements: A skill is a directory named for the skill (e.g., competitor-analysis/) containing a required skill.markdown (or SKILL.md) file, plus optional supporting files.
correct_answers.storage_location: Skills are stored in version-controlled repositories — not in chat windows or shared documents — making them auditable, updatable, and recoverable.

activities.instruction: Match each element to the correct skill file part (frontmatter or body) before continuing.

activities.section_title: The Routing Description: Your Most Important Line
activities.instruction: Identify what is wrong with this description and name the correct fix before continuing.

items.text:
description: "Assists enterprise analysts with competitive analysis and market overviews
and pricing summaries."

correct_answers.primary_flaw: The description spans two lines. LLM routing parsers read the description as a single-line token; the second line is invisible to the parser.
correct_answers.fix: Collapse to one continuous line with no newline characters.
correct_answers.secondary_flaw: 'Assists with' is a vague summary with no concrete trigger phrases or output shape.

activities.section_title: The Five Subsystems of a Skill Body
activities.instruction: Match each subsystem to its primary purpose before continuing.

purposes.text: Replaces default model hallucination with defined policy at constraint boundaries
Keeps the file under 150 lines to prevent context overrun
Encodes decision principles that allow generalization to unanticipated situations
Provides few-shot reference output to anchor model generation
Declares exact output schema for reliable downstream parsing

activities.instruction: Classify each statement as the correct reason for the 150-line constraint or an incorrect reason, before continuing.

items.text: Enterprise LLM gateways inject surrounding context, which can push a long skill past the model's attention horizon.
Longer files are harder for humans to read.
Version control systems have file size limits.
The constraint forces clarity — if you can't say it in 150 lines, the skill is probably doing too much.

activities.instruction: Cover the section and write the complete description formula template from memory before continuing.
prompts: What are the required components of the description formula?
Write one compliant example description using the formula.

correct_answers.formula: description: "Triggers on [TRIGGER PHRASE 1] or [TRIGGER PHRASE 2] to [ACTION VERB] [OUTPUT ARTIFACT]: [SCHEMA DETAILS]. [KEY CONSTRAINTS]."
correct_answers.example_note: Any compliant example accepted. E.g.: "Triggers on 'summarize meeting' or 'meeting notes' to output a structured markdown summary: Attendees | Key Decisions | Action Items (owner + due date). Maximum 300 words."

activities.section_title: Worked Example: Building a Skill From Scratch
activities.instruction: Put the six steps for building a skill from scratch in the correct order before continuing.

items.text: Write the Output Specification
Add a Pattern-Matching Exemplar
Define the trigger and output in plain English
Write the Reasoning Framework
Document Edge Cases
Write the description (one line)

activities.section_title: Common Mistakes and How to Fix Them
activities.instruction: Identify which named mistake pattern each example represents before continuing.

items.text: description: "Helps users with their meetings and notes."

## Instructions
Step 1: Read the input.
Step 2: Find names.
Step 3: Find verbs near names.
## Output\nWrite up the action items you find in the transcript.

correct_answers.explanation: No semantic anchor; matches almost everything.
Step-by-step instructions break at unanticipated situations.
No schema; downstream agents cannot parse consistently.

activities.instruction: Classify each element as required or optional in a production-grade skill before continuing.

items.text: A single-line description with concrete trigger phrases
A Reasoning Framework
A Deterministic Output Specification
Three or more Edge Cases
One Example Output (exemplar)
A file under 150 lines

correct_answers.note: All six are required per the assignment rubric for a production-grade skill. The exemplar (E) may be inline or a separate file.

modules.title: Agentic Contracts: Skills Built for Autonomous Agents

activities.section_title: The Shift From Human-Directed to Agent-Directed Skills
activities.instruction: Match each phrase to the correct analogy target (recipe or API) before continuing.

phrases.text: A human cook adjusting with judgment
An automated pipeline that crashes on ambiguous input

activities.instruction: Match each SLA Contract block to what it declares before continuing.

declarations.text: What the calling agent can reliably expect from this skill's output
Upstream preconditions (encoding, format, quality) that must hold
Required and optional input fields, types, formats, and constraints
All possible output schemas including success paths and STATUS_CODE failure states

activities.section_title: Worked Example: Human Skill vs. Agentic Skill
activities.instruction: The agentic skill output contract reads: 'If no items found, explain politely that the query returned nothing.' Identify the flaw before continuing.

items.text: Output Contract excerpt: 'If no action items found, explain politely that the query returned no results.'

correct_answers.flaw: Natural language failure messages cannot be parsed by downstream agents.
correct_answers.fix: Failure states must be STATUS_CODEs (e.g., STATUS: NO_ITEMS_FOUND), not prose explanations.

activities.instruction: Cover the section and write the definition of composability and name the most dangerous property of composability failures before continuing.
prompts: What makes composability failures especially dangerous?

correct_answers.composability_definition: The property where the output of Skill A natively serves as the input to Skill B without human intervention, data transformation, or re-prompting.
correct_answers.danger: Composability failures are silent — the pipeline continues running with subtly corrupted data, producing plausible but wrong results.

activities.instruction: Skill Alpha outputs 3-field pipe-delimited rows: owner│action│due_date_iso. Skill Beta expects 4-field rows: owner│action│due_date_iso│urgency_score. Identify the design flaw and the correct fix before continuing.

items.text: Skill Alpha Output Contract: pipe-delimited rows: owner│action│due_date_iso
Skill Beta Input Contract:  pipe-delimited rows: owner│action│due_date_iso│urgency_score

correct_answers.flaw: Schema mismatch — Alpha outputs 3 fields, Beta expects 4. Beta receives malformed input on every row.
correct_answers.fix: Add a placeholder urgency_score field (value: 0) to Skill Alpha's output so the schema is always 4 fields. Beta reads urgency_score == 0 as 'unscored' and populates it.

activities.instruction: Put the three pipeline stages in the correct processing order before continuing.

items.text: SKILL BETA: urgency-scorer — populates urgency_score 1-5, sorts output
INPUT: Raw meeting transcript (plain text, UTF-8)
SKILL ALPHA: meeting-action-extractor-v2 — outputs owner│action│due_date_iso│urgency_score(=0)

activities.section_title: When NOT to Use a Skill: The Deterministic Script Rule
activities.instruction: Classify each task as 'Use a Skill' or 'Use a Script' before continuing.

items.text: Extracting named entities from a legal document
Converting a date string from ISO 8601 to US format
Scoring customer support tickets by urgency
Inserting a validated record into a production database
Summarizing variable-length quarterly reports
Sending a confirmation email via SMTP

correct_answers.script_rationale: Require exact, deterministic execution with no variance

modules.title: Debugging Skills and Identifying Failure Modes

activities.instruction: Match each symptom to the correct failure type before continuing.

symptoms.text: Skill never invokes despite a matching user query; agent uses default behavior
Skill invokes correctly but output structure varies between runs; downstream parsing intermittently fails
Upstream skill succeeds; downstream skill receives input but emits INVALID_INPUT silently

activities.instruction: Put the first four debugging checklist items in the correct order before continuing.

items.text: Do all failure paths emit machine-parseable STATUS_CODEs?
Is the description on exactly one line? (Open the raw file, not a rendered preview)
Is the Output Spec entirely deterministic? Remove any prose-style output instructions.
Do the trigger phrases match the actual language the calling agent uses?

activities.instruction: Cover the section and state the single question that governance answers for every skill before continuing.
prompts: What is the one governance question that applies to every skill in a repository?
Name three specific problems that an ungoverned skills repository creates.

correct_answers.governance_question: Who owns it, who can change it, and where does it live?
problems_any_three_of: Critical single points of failure when the author leaves the organization

activities.instruction: Match each skill example to its correct governance tier before continuing.
Put the four tier promotion steps in the correct order before continuing.

items.text: Validate: the domain expert who used the skill reviews the generalized version for correctness.
Identify: team leads review Tier 3 skills quarterly for patterns that indicate shared value.
Promote: the skill moves to the team repository and governance controls apply.
Generalize: the skill is rewritten to remove personal idiosyncrasies.

modules.title: Quantitative Testing: Why Manual Testing Fails

activities.instruction: Cover the section and explain in one sentence why changing a single word in one skill can degrade an unrelated skill before continuing.
prompts: Why does a vocabulary change in Skill A affect the output of Skill B?
What does manual testing miss that quantitative regression testing catches?

correct_answers.mechanism: Transformers process the entire skills context as a unified semantic field; a vocabulary change shifts probability distributions across all downstream token generation — including adjacent skills sharing the same context.
correct_answers.manual_vs_quantitative: Manual testing catches only the changed skill. Quantitative regression testing catches cross-skill effects from context sensitivity.

activities.instruction: Classify each rubric criterion as objective (acceptable) or subjective (not acceptable) before continuing.

items.text: 'Score 5 if the output matches schema exactly: all fields present, correct sort order, no extra text.'
'Score 5 if the response looks well-structured and professional.'
'Score 3 if manual field extraction is possible but automation requires additional handling.'
'Score 3 if it seems mostly right.'

correct_answers.objective_rationale: Verifiable by a parser or structural inspection
correct_answers.subjective_rationale: Cannot be objectively verified; introduce evaluator bias

activities.instruction: Match each test case description to its correct test type before continuing.

test_cases.text: 500-word transcript, 3 clear action items, all owners named, all dates explicit
Input is a recipe, not a meeting transcript
Transcript where two names appear near the same verb
A test run on the Tier 1 brand voice skill after updating the action extractor

modules.title: Enterprise Deployment: Continuity, Version Control, and Scale

activities.instruction: Match each repository directory to its correct owner/purpose before continuing.

owners_purposes.text: v1 scores used for regression comparison

activities.instruction: Match each continuity risk to its correct mitigation before continuing.

mitigations.text: Policy requiring all skills committed to team repository; enforced via offboarding checklist
Quarterly documentation reviews, skill promotion review, README files per skill
Automated regression test suite running on every model update; rollback playbook

pairs.explanation: Skill Orphaning → documentation reviews + promotion process
Tier-3 Loss → commit policy + offboarding enforcement
